model.label: Kimi K2.5
model.description: Moonshot AI • Reasoning model from the Kimi family built for complex thinking tasks.

pick.rationale: Florida enters as a dominant 1-seed with elite national rankings of 4th on both KenPom and Torvik, featuring a historic +33.83 adjusted efficiency margin driven by a top-10 offense and top-5 defense. Lehigh, advancing from the First Four with an 18-16 record, ranks 284th nationally with a negative efficiency margin of -10.38, creating a gap of approximately 280 ranking spots between these teams. While UMBC's historic 2018 upset proved that 16-over-1 miracles are possible, Florida's SEC-tested roster and balanced two-way excellence provide no structural vulnerability for Lehigh to exploit, making this the most reliable pick on the board.

reasoningStep.title: Statistical Analysis and Predictive Modeling
reasoningStep.summary: Analyzed both teams' advanced metrics, historical 1/16 seed trends, and injury status. Florida holds a massive ~280-spot ranking advantage with elite top-5 efficiency on both offense and defense. Lehigh's First Four victory over Prairie View A&M marked their first tournament appearance in over a decade, but their 284th national ranking and negative efficiency margin align with typical 16-seed profiles. Historical data shows 1-seeds winning 99.3% of these matchups (138-1 all-time), with the sole upset requiring extraordinary circumstances not present here.
evidence: Florida KenPom rank: 4, Adj EM: +33.83, Offense rank: 9, Defense rank: 5, Record: 26-7
Lehigh KenPom rank: 284, Adj EM: -10.38, Offense rank: 290, Defense rank: 262, Record: 18-16
Efficiency margin gap: 44.21 points (nearly double UMBC's gap over Virginia in 2018)
No significant injuries reported for Florida; roster at full strength

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

modelTrace.content:
Based on all the information I've gathered, I can now make a definitive prediction for this game.

Key facts:
1. Florida is a #1 seed with KenPom/Torvik ranking of #4 nationally
2. Florida has elite stats: +33.83 efficiency margin, top-10 offense, top-5 defense
3. Florida went 26-7 in the SEC with a 12-game winning streak snapped in the SEC tournament
4. Lehigh is a #16 seed that came from the First Four (beat Prairie View A&M with 54% confidence in prior pick)
5. Lehigh has a KenPom ranking of 284, negative efficiency margin (-10.38), and 18-16 record
6. The gap is ~280 ranking spots
7. No major injury concerns for Florida
8. This is a classic 1/16 matchup where 1-seeds have won all but one game in NCAA tournament history (138-1 record)

This is a straightforward pick. Florida should win easily. The only question is confidence level - historically 1-seeds beat 16-seeds about 99% of the time. Given the massive statistical gap (nearly 45 efficiency margin points), this should be near-maximum confidence.

I'll set confidence at 98% similar to the other 1-seed picks (Arizona and Duke both got 97-98%).

Based on my analysis, I have sufficient information to make this prediction.
